json.fix_description: In the PEG grammar's `identifier` rule in `libs/modkit-odata/src/odata_parse.rs`, change the trailing identifier-character repetition from one-or-more (`+`) to zero-or-more (`*`) while keeping the existing first-character rule unchanged. Keep or add the regression test `single_character_identifier` in `libs/modkit-odata/src/odata_parse_tests.rs` asserting that `parse_str("x eq 1")` returns `Expr::Compare(Expr::Identifier("x"), Equal, Expr::Value(Value::Number(1)))`.
json.reasoning: The reproduction test calls `parse_str("x eq 1")` and fails with `Parsing("error at 1:2: expected ['a'..='z'|'A'..='Z'|'_'|'0'..='9']")`, which points exactly to the parser expecting an additional identifier character after `x`. Multi-character identifiers already work because they satisfy the existing `+` suffix. The smallest correct fix is to leave the valid first-character set intact and relax only the suffix repetition to `*`, allowing one-character identifiers while preserving support for longer identifiers and continuing to reject identifiers that start with digits.
json.root_cause: The `identifier` rule at `libs/modkit-odata/src/odata_parse.rs:98-99` requires a first identifier character followed by `['a'..='z'|'A'..='Z'|'_'|'0'..='9']+`. Because `+` means one or more, the grammar accidentally makes the minimum identifier length two characters. When parsing `x eq 1`, the parser consumes the initial `x` as the first character and then fails at column 2 because the next character is a space instead of another identifier character.
json.summary_markdown:
## Bug
`cf-modkit-odata` rejects valid filters whose field identifier has one character, such as `x eq 1`.

## Cause
The PEG `identifier` rule in `libs/modkit-odata/src/odata_parse.rs:98-99` requires a valid first character plus one or more additional identifier characters. That makes the effective minimum identifier length two, so `x eq 1` fails at the space after `x`.

## Fix
Change only the suffix repetition in the `identifier` rule from `+` to `*`, keeping the first-character restriction unchanged. Retain the regression test in `libs/modkit-odata/src/odata_parse_tests.rs` for `parse_str("x eq 1")` producing an equality comparison against numeric value `1`.

## Verification
Focused reproduction command is currently red before the fix:

```sh
cargo test -p cf-modkit-odata --lib single_character_identifier
```

After the grammar edit, this focused test should pass; then run `cargo test -p cf-modkit-odata --lib` to check the parser library suite.

Confidence: 0.98
